claim: The explorer claim is confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has boundary snapshot auditing (SHA256-based file fingerprinting), agent tool profile support (boundary/research/full normalization), and project-mutation detection (before/after snapshot diff) implemented in evidence-dispatch.ts with PRD readiness blocking on violations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA256 digests of all project files except allowed write roots
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit uses cryptographic file fingerprinting to detect mutations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshots and returns added/modified/deleted changes
evidence.why: Verifies the actual mutation detection logic that catches violations

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution; runs diff to detect mutations
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit is actually executed during agent runs, not just defined

evidence.summary: Boundary violations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers to readiness and set prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary violations actually block PRD readiness as claimed

evidence.summary: Claude provider normalizes agent_tool_profile to research/full/boundary; disables session persistence and clears tool overrides
evidence.why: Confirms tool profile normalization implementation and that evidence agents are stateless

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines project_mutation=forbidden and allowed_write_roots as output directory only
evidence.why: Verifies the boundary control policy is defined in dispatch plan

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes boundary rules: do not modify target project files, allowed artifact root
evidence.why: Confirms natural language boundary instructions are included in agent prompts

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing() filters out status items like no missing data identified from missing arrays
evidence.why: Confirms missing validation logic that prevents false positive blockers

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates evidence records declare scope as project/external/user/unknown
evidence.why: Verifies schema validation enforces evidence scope requirements
Confirms protocol definitions that should forbid project mutations

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks for required evidence agent roles and scope issues
evidence.why: Verifies PRD readiness logic validates evidence agent completeness

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() supports claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools CLI flags
evidence.why: Confirms tool restriction mechanism exists in provider adapter but evidence-dispatch does not use it

risks: Boundary snapshot audit has no test coverage or stress test evidence showing it actually catches mutations
Agent tool profile full grants all Claude Code tools including Write; prompt-level boundary instruction may not override tool availability at runtime
No evidence that provider-adapter claude_allowed_tools actually integrates with evidence-dispatch boundary policy
If allowed_write_roots resolution fails, artifacts might write into project directory and bypass detection
Protocol writes_code=false is declarative only; no enforcement mechanism prevents tool use at Claude Code runtime
missing: No test evidence that boundary snapshot audit detects mutations in practice
No integration evidence showing agent_tool_profile full agents actually respect prompt boundary constraints
No evidence that claude_allowed_tools/disallowed_tools from provider-adapter are used by evidence-dispatch
No stress test evidence with real Claude -p full-tool agents attempting mutations
No evidence that boundary audit handles edge cases like symlink changes, permission changes, or timestamp-only changes

result.notes: Independently confirmed all implementation claims from explorer. Line numbers and summaries are accurate. However, the critical gap remains: no evidence that the boundary enforcement actually works in practice with real Claude agents, especially full-tool agents where prompt instructions may not override tool permissions.
